channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Candles and tablecloth at White Castle: How a Valentine’s Day tradition sprouted over 30 years ago and spread nationwide
item.description: The "fun and quirky" promotion started in 1991 and was only offered at White Castles in St. Louis and Minneapolis.

item.title: The hidden costs of becoming an expat
item.description: Since I was old enough to vote in presidential elections, I’ve heard plenty of grumbling across the political spectrum about moving to Canada if one c...

item.title: Americans wake up and smell the coffee price surge—skipping Starbucks, brewing at home, and drinking Diet Coke for caffeine
item.description: Coffee prices in the U.S. were up 18.3% in January from a year ago. Over five years, coffee prices rose 47%.

item.title: A U.S. ‘debt spiral’ could start in coming years when the interest rate on government borrowing exceeds economic growth, budget watchdog says
item.description: CRFB warned the actual fiscal outlook could be far worse than even the latest sobering projections.

item.title: The ‘Zombie Internet’ Has Arrived, Bringing Devastating Consequences to These Sectors
item.description: It’s like the dead internet, only more extreme. Here’s why the arrival of Moltbook signifies the start of a strange and unsettling new era in tech.

item.title: 3 Ways to Negotiate Better Business by Understanding the Other Side
item.description: If you want to get a better deal, treat everyone with respect.

item.title: Victorian-era ‘vinegar valentines’ show that trolling existed long before social media or the internet
item.description: In 1885, a resident of Birmingham, UK, was charged with the attempted murder of his estranged wife after he received a vinegar valentine from her.

item.title: The Worst Michelin Meal Ever Reveals a Hidden Truth About Creativity
item.description: Here’s how science, art, and even Michelin-star disasters disprove a common myth about creativity.

item.title: Gen Z’s latest revolt over Jeffrey Epstein: pointing out a connection to the company that takes class photos
item.description: Some school districts are cancelling school photos as the company, Lifetouch, called the claims “completely false.”

item.title: Peacock’s new feature lets you sit courtside at the NBA All-Star Game
item.description: For decades, tuning into a sporting event at home involved watching a traditional broadcast on your TV. These days, however, many viewers aren’t just ...

item.title: How to Translate Strategy Into Daily Execution
item.description: When leaders choose alignment consistently, it pays off.

item.title: This classic MTV website goes where Netflix dare not venture
item.description: Call it the day the music died. On December 31, 2025, MTV’s last music-only stations shut down forever​ . The last video played on MTV Music in the U....

item.title: Eddie Bauer Plans to Close All U.S. and Canada Stores in Bankruptcy. See the Full List of Doomed Locations
item.description: The embattled outdoor retailer has already closed 49 locations. Closing sales at remaining stores are expected to be complete by May unless a buyer is...

item.title: Homebuilder lot supply jumps so fast that 2 housing markets are now ‘significantly oversupplied’
item.description: Want more housing market stories from Lance Lambert’s ResiClub in your inbox? Subscribe to the ResiClub newsletter . During the pandemic housing boom,...

item.title: With one word, Travis Kelce may have (unintentionally) revealed his retirement plans
item.description: Will Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce retire after this football season ? Kelce has not yet delivered a public answer to this question, and t...

item.title: How a riptide around AI and stocks could affect Fed decisions and housing market
These ‘safer’ chip stocks have boomed this year. Is it too late to buy in?
Tax refunds are up this year, early IRS numbers show. See how yours compares.
Who needs chocolates? Vacuums and car tires are the new Valentine's Day treats.
You can now use buy-now-pay-later to pay your rent — but watch out for the fees
